computation_name: MinHash LSH Candidate Generation for Jaccard Similarity
definition: Sketch each set with MinHash, use locality-sensitive hashing bands to propose candidate train neighbors, then compute exact Jaccard or Tanimoto only for candidates. This preserves a transparent set-overlap definition while reducing the pair count.

scale_profile.best_for: Large set-valued data where exact Jaccard over all train-eval pairs is too expensive.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions of sparse sets, depending on token count and banding choices.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Low-similarity tails are scientifically important and LSH would intentionally drop them.

tunable_parameters.name: k
tunable_parameters.purpose: Number of exact reranked candidates retained.

quality_gates: Measure candidate recall against exact Jaccard on a subset.
Use exact reranking before emitting final similarity values.
failure_modes: Banding can drop true moderate-similarity neighbors.
Tokenization choices can dominate the apparent spectral axis.
Empty or tiny sets can create unstable similarity estimates.
example_usage: python minhash_lsh_jaccard.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --set-col kmers --num-hashes 128 --rows-per-band 4 --top-k 50

citations.title: SuperMinHash - A New Minwise Hashing Algorithm for Jaccard Similarity Estimation
citations.evidence: Discusses MinHash signatures for fast Jaccard estimation and the cost of direct pairwise Jaccard at scale.

citations.title: A Survey on Locality Sensitive Hashing Algorithms and their Applications
citations.evidence: Surveys LSH methods used to reduce candidate comparisons in similarity search.
